name: AI Receptionist
tagline: Production-grade WhatsApp AI that handles dental clinic conversations end-to-end.
highlights: Intent Router Agent with 6 distinct workflow paths
Hybrid RAG with semantic cache — eliminates redundant LLM calls
Full booking, rescheduling, and cancellation via Cal.com
Human escalation via Brevo API with full context handoff
Persistent conversation state in PostgreSQL across sessions

nodes.label: WhatsApp Webhook
nodes.description: Receives inbound WhatsApp messages via webhook. Extracts user query and session metadata, then forwards to the Intent Router Agent.

nodes.label: Intent Router Agent
nodes.description: Classifies user intent and routes to the correct workflow: dental FAQ → RAG, booking → Booking Workflow, emergency → Human Escalation, off-topic → graceful refusal, cancel → Cancel Workflow, reschedule → Reschedule Workflow. Reads persistent conversation state from PostgreSQL.

nodes.label: PostgreSQL State
nodes.description: Stores full conversation history and session state. The Intent Router reads this on every turn to maintain multi-turn context without re-sending history.

nodes.label: FAQ Workflow
nodes.description: Ingests PDF/DOCX knowledge base: parses → chunks → embeds. On query: checks Semantic Cache first (hit = instant response). On miss: Hybrid RAG (vector + keyword search) → LLM → response → cache write.

nodes.label: Booking Workflow
nodes.description: Booking Agent collects date, time, and service. Validation node checks for missing fields and asks follow-up questions. Once complete, checks Cal.com availability and books the slot. Emails supervisor via Brevo if API is down.

nodes.label: Reschedule Workflow
nodes.description: Retrieves existing booking from conversation state. Collects new date/time preferences. Validates, checks availability, and updates the Cal.com slot. Mirrors Booking Agent logic with reschedule-specific state transitions.

nodes.label: Cancellation Workflow
nodes.description: Retrieves active appointment from conversation state. Sends cancellation request to Cal.com via Python. If Cal.com API is down, emails supervisor for manual cancellation. Updates patient record in PostgreSQL.

nodes.label: Human Escalation
nodes.description: Triggered on emergency conditions or off-topic queries that can't be handled gracefully. Passes full conversation context to a human agent via Brevo API. Ensures no context loss during handoff.

nodes.label: Cal.com API
nodes.description: Checks slot availability and creates calendar bookings. Returns booking confirmation details which are relayed back to the patient via WhatsApp.

nodes.label: Cal.com API
nodes.description: Updates existing bookings with new date/time. Cancels old slot and creates new one atomically.

nodes.label: PostgreSQL (Patients, Appointments)
nodes.description: Central database storing patient records and all appointment data. Every workflow writes back here — bookings, reschedules, cancellations all update the patient record.

engineeringDecisions.rationale: Six distinct intent paths needed explicit state machine control. Unstructured agents loop unpredictably in production — LangGraph gives deterministic routing with full state persistence.

Dental FAQs cluster tightly — patients ask the same questions repeatedly. A cosine similarity cache with threshold 0.92 eliminates the majority of redundant LLM calls before they hit the vector store.
Dental terminology like procedure names and medical codes fail pure semantic search. BM25 keyword retrieval captures exact matches that embeddings miss.
When Cal.com is down, the system doesn't fail silently — it emails the supervisor immediately so no booking request is lost.

challenges.title: Multi-turn state across 6 workflows
challenges.description: Each workflow needed isolated state but shared conversation history. LangGraph's checkpointer with PostgreSQL backend solved state isolation without losing cross-turn context.

challenges.title: Cal.com API downtime handling
challenges.description: Production requires graceful degradation. Added Brevo email fallback so any booking/cancellation request that can't be processed automatically gets escalated to a human immediately.

name: Geopolitical Newsletter Agent
tagline: Autonomous research pipeline. From 15 articles to a validated, published newsletter in 15 seconds.
highlights: Tavily pulls up to 15 articles from last 24 hours
LLM scores articles 1-10, selects top 6 by relevance
Trafilatura extracts clean, ad-free article text from HTML
Reflection node validates newsletter against source articles
Max 2 retry loops before force-publish — no infinite loops

pipeline.description: Tavily pulls up to 15 geopolitical articles from the last 24 hours
LLM Score Node: rates articles 1-10 on specified factors, returns top 6 links
Trafilatura: extracts main article content from HTML — clean, ad-free text
LLM Newsletter Generator Node using Jinja2 HTML template
LLM Reflection Node: validates newsletter accuracy against extracted source text
should_continue(): publish if score ≥ threshold OR retry ≥ 2, else revise
Django EmailMessage sends newsletter to Recipients List (DB) as background task

nodes.label: Tavily Search
nodes.description: Pulls up to 15 geopolitical news articles from the last 24 hours. Returns article URLs, titles, and snippets for scoring.

nodes.label: LLM Score Node
nodes.description: LLM scores each article 1-10 based on specified factors (recency, geopolitical relevance, source authority). Returns the top 6 links for extraction. Eliminates noise before expensive extraction.

nodes.label: Trafilatura Extractor
nodes.description: Fetches each of the top 6 article URLs and extracts clean, ad-free main content from HTML. Trafilatura outperforms BeautifulSoup for news articles by stripping navigation, ads, and boilerplate.

nodes.label: Jinja2 HTML Template
nodes.description: Pre-built HTML newsletter template injected by the generator node. Ensures consistent formatting, branding, and structure across every edition.

nodes.label: LLM Newsletter Generator
nodes.description: Generates the newsletter from clean article text using the Jinja2 HTML template. Produces structured sections: headlines, analysis, and key takeaways.

nodes.label: LLM Reflection Node
nodes.description: Validates the generated newsletter against the original extracted article text. Checks for hallucinations, factual accuracy, and tone consistency. Returns a quality verdict.

nodes.label: Router: should_continue()
nodes.description: Deterministic router: if status == 'publish' OR iter ≥ 2 → send to publisher (end). If status == 'revise' AND iter < 2 → loop back to generator. Hard cap at 2 retries prevents infinite loops.

nodes.label: Recipients List (DB)
nodes.description: PostgreSQL table storing all newsletter subscribers. The publisher queries this to build the recipient list for each edition.

nodes.label: Django EmailMessage
nodes.description: Sends the approved newsletter as a formatted HTML email to all recipients fetched from the DB. Runs as a Django background task to avoid blocking the main pipeline.

engineeringDecisions.rationale: Trafilatura extraction is expensive per URL. Scoring first with just snippets filters 15 articles to 6 before any full fetch — 60% reduction in extraction cost with negligible scoring overhead.
BeautifulSoup extracts everything including ads and nav. Trafilatura is purpose-built for news article extraction and produces significantly cleaner text, which directly improves generation quality.
Reflection loops without a cap create infinite revision cycles in production. A hard cap of 2 retries ensures the pipeline always terminates, accepting slightly lower quality over system failure.
Email delivery blocks the main pipeline thread for multiple seconds. Running as a Django background task allows the pipeline to return immediately while delivery happens async.

challenges.title: Paywalled article content
challenges.description: Trafilatura returns empty on paywalled URLs. Added a content validation step after extraction that removes empty articles from the set before generation, preventing hallucination from missing context.

challenges.title: Reflection loop quality threshold
challenges.description: Calibrating the quality threshold for the reflection node required testing across 50+ editions. Too strict caused near-infinite revisions; too lenient allowed low-quality output through.

name: Meeting Intelligence Platform
tagline: Drop a Zoom recording. Get a structured intelligence report — decisions, actions, risks — in your inbox.
highlights: Two ingestion paths: API upload (prod) and Zoom watchdog (local dev)
Whisper large v3 via Groq for high-accuracy transcription
LangGraph workflow: Report Generation → Persist → Deliver
Recipients fetched from Supabase, emailed BCC for privacy

nodes.label: Audio Upload API
nodes.description: Production path: accepts audio file upload via API (e.g. USD-prefixed filename). Passes file path to the process_audio() function which handles transcription.

nodes.label: Zoom Watchdog
nodes.description: Local dev path: Python watchdog monitors a directory for new Zoom recordings. When a new file appears, it automatically triggers the pipeline — no manual upload needed.

nodes.label: process_audio()
nodes.description: Production transcription function. Takes file_path, runs Whisper large v3 via Groq for speech-to-text. Audio file is deleted after transcription to avoid storage accumulation.

nodes.label: Audio → Speech-to-text
nodes.description: Local dev transcription path. Same Whisper large v3 via Groq model as production. Produces identical transcript format so both paths feed seamlessly into the same LangGraph workflow.

nodes.label: LangGraph Workflow
nodes.description: process_transcript() entry point. Orchestrates the three-node LangGraph workflow: Report Generation → Persist Meeting Report → Report Delivery. Both ingestion paths converge here.

nodes.label: Report Generation Node
nodes.description: Qwen3-32B via Groq generates a structured intelligence report from the full transcript. Extracts decisions, action items, owners, deadlines, risks, blockers, and meeting summary.

nodes.label: Persist Meeting Report
nodes.description: Saves both the raw transcription and the generated intelligence report to PostgreSQL (Supabase). Enables historical lookup and future search over past meetings.

nodes.label: Recipient Registration API
nodes.description: API for registering meeting report recipients. Writes email addresses to the PostgreSQL Recipients table in Supabase.

nodes.label: PostgreSQL (Supabase)
nodes.description: Supabase-hosted PostgreSQL. Stores raw transcripts, intelligence reports, and the recipients list. The delivery node fetches recipients from here.

nodes.label: Report Delivery (Email)
nodes.description: Fetches recipients from Supabase and sends the intelligence report via email. Uses BCC for privacy so recipients don't see each other's addresses.

engineeringDecisions.rationale: Groq's hardware acceleration runs Whisper large v3 significantly faster than OpenAI's API while maintaining the same accuracy. Transcription of a 60-min meeting completes in under 30s.
Qwen3-32B outperforms smaller models on long-context structured extraction tasks. Meeting transcripts can be 10,000+ tokens — model quality at this context length matters more than speed.
Production needs a clean API endpoint; local development needs zero friction. The watchdog path enables rapid testing without building upload tooling. Both paths produce identical transcript format.
Meeting participants shouldn't see who else received the report. BCC ensures each recipient gets a private copy without exposing the full distribution list.

challenges.title: Variable Zoom audio quality
challenges.description: Zoom recordings vary wildly in quality based on network conditions. Added a pre-transcription quality check — files below a SNR threshold get flagged for manual review rather than producing garbage transcripts.

challenges.title: Long transcript context
challenges.description: Hour-long meetings produce 10,000+ token transcripts. Tested multiple models — Qwen3-32B was the only model that consistently extracted all 12+ fields correctly without hallucinating on long contexts.
